Mary Lamar Rickey  (1938-10-27)October 27, 1938  [Knoxville, Tennessee](/wiki/Knoxville,_Tennessee "Knoxville, Tennessee"), U.S.
October 12, 2023(2023-10-12) (aged 84)  [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), U.S.
[Rhodes College](/wiki/Rhodes_College "Rhodes College")  [University of Iowa](/wiki/University_of_Iowa "University of Iowa")

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Lara+Parker%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Lara+Parker%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Lara+Parker%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Lara+Parker%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(October 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(October 27, 1938 – October 12, 2023), better known as **Lara Parker**, was an American actress and writer known for her role as the witch [Angelique Bouchard Collins](/wiki/Angelique_Bouchard_Collins "Angelique Bouchard Collins") on the [ABC-TV](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") serial *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows "Dark Shadows")*, which aired from 1966 to 1971.

Tate, Marsh Ann; Houser, Earl (2022). [*What America Watched: Television Favorites from the Cornfields to the Cosmos, 1960s-1990s*](https://books.google.com/books?id=aD1ZEAAAQBAJ&dq=Lara+Parker+1938&pg=PA157). McFarland. p. 157. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9781476644653

**Mary Lamar Rickey** (October 27, 1938 – October 12, 2023), better known as **Lara Parker**, was an American actress and writer known for her role as the witch [Angelique Bouchard Collins](/wiki/Angelique_Bouchard_Collins "Angelique Bouchard Collins") on the [ABC-TV](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") serial *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows "Dark Shadows")*, which aired from 1966 to 1971.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Lara Parker was born in [Knoxville, Tennessee](/wiki/Knoxville,_Tennessee "Knoxville, Tennessee"), on October 27, 1938,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and grew up in [Memphis](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee"). Descendant of a prominent Southern family, she was a great-great-granddaughter of [Lucius Quintus Cincinnatus Lamar II](/wiki/Lucius_Quintus_Cincinnatus_Lamar "Lucius Quintus Cincinnatus Lamar") and a third-great-granddaughter of [Augustus Baldwin Longstreet](/wiki/Augustus_Baldwin_Longstreet "Augustus Baldwin Longstreet"), an uncle of Confederate General [James Longstreet](/wiki/James_Longstreet "James Longstreet"). She began a philosophy A.B. at [Vassar College](/wiki/Vassar_College "Vassar College") before receiving her [Bachelor of Arts](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts") degree from [Rhodes College](/wiki/Rhodes_College "Rhodes College") (then known as Southwestern Presbyterian) and a [Master of Arts](/wiki/Master_of_Arts "Master of Arts") degree in speech and drama from the [University of Iowa](/wiki/University_of_Iowa "University of Iowa").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Parker's best known role was as the witch [Angelique Bouchard Collins](/wiki/Angelique_Bouchard_Collins "Angelique Bouchard Collins") on the supernatural [soap opera](/wiki/Soap_opera "Soap opera") *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows "Dark Shadows")* from 1966 to 1971. She later played the role of Laura Banner in the opening sequence of the pilot for the television series *[The Incredible Hulk](/wiki/The_Incredible_Hulk_(1978_TV_series) "The Incredible Hulk (1978 TV series)")* (1977), and the fashion model-witch Madelaine in the *[Kolchak: The Night Stalker](/wiki/Kolchak:_The_Night_Stalker "Kolchak: The Night Stalker")* episode "The Trevi Collection". Her other television work includes appearances on the *[Emergency](/wiki/Emergency "Emergency")* fifth season episode "One of Those Days" (1975), *[Kung Fu](/wiki/Kung_Fu_(1972_TV_series) "Kung Fu (1972 TV series)")*, *[The Six Million Dollar Man](/wiki/The_Six_Million_Dollar_Man "The Six Million Dollar Man")* second season episode "The Deadly Replay" (1974), *[Police Woman](/wiki/Police_Woman_(TV_series) "Police Woman (TV series)")*, *[Kojak](/wiki/Kojak "Kojak")*, *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")*, *[Quincy, M.E.](/wiki/Quincy,_M.E. "Quincy, M.E.")*, *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*, *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")*, *[Highway to Heaven](/wiki/Highway_to_Heaven "Highway to Heaven")*, *[Switch](/wiki/Switch_(American_TV_series) "Switch (American TV series)")*, *[Baretta](/wiki/Baretta "Baretta")*, *[Galactica 1980](/wiki/Galactica_1980 "Galactica 1980")* ("The Night The Cylons Landed", Parts I & II), the *Barnaby Jones* episode "The Price of Anger" (1980), the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") daytime serial *[Capitol](/wiki/Capitol_(TV_series) "Capitol (TV series)")*, and the [ABC](/wiki/American_Broadcasting_Company "American Broadcasting Company") daytime serial *[One Life to Live](/wiki/One_Life_to_Live "One Life to Live")*. She played secretary Wanda in the 1977 television miniseries *[Washington: Behind Closed Doors](/wiki/Washington:_Behind_Closed_Doors "Washington: Behind Closed Doors")* and had a recurring role in the short-lived television series *[Jessica Novak](/wiki/Jessica_Novak "Jessica Novak")*.

In 1971, Parker reprised the role of Angelique in *[Night of Dark Shadows](/wiki/Night_of_Dark_Shadows "Night of Dark Shadows")*, the second feature film based on *Dark Shadows*. She was joined by her *Dark Shadows* castmates [Kate Jackson](/wiki/Kate_Jackson "Kate Jackson"), [David Selby](/wiki/David_Selby "David Selby"), [Grayson Hall](/wiki/Grayson_Hall "Grayson Hall"), [Nancy Barrett](/wiki/Nancy_Barrett "Nancy Barrett"), [John Karlen](/wiki/John_Karlen "John Karlen"), and [Thayer David](/wiki/Thayer_David "Thayer David"). This film was more loosely based on the series than *[House of Dark Shadows](/wiki/House_of_Dark_Shadows "House of Dark Shadows")* was the year before and it did not fare as well at the box office as the first film.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She appeared opposite [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro") in the 1970 [Brian De Palma](/wiki/Brian_De_Palma "Brian De Palma") film *[Hi, Mom!](/wiki/Hi,_Mom! "Hi, Mom!")*, but her best known film role came in the [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards")-winning drama *[Save the Tiger](/wiki/Save_the_Tiger "Save the Tiger")* (1973), starring [Jack Lemmon](/wiki/Jack_Lemmon "Jack Lemmon"), in which she played Margo, a sympathetic prostitute who is devastated when her client Fred Mirrell ([Norman Burton](/wiki/Norman_Burton "Norman Burton")) suffers a near fatal [heart attack](/wiki/Myocardial_infarction "Myocardial infarction"). In 1975, she played Kelly Marsh, the wife of [Peter Fonda](/wiki/Peter_Fonda "Peter Fonda")'s character Roger in *[Race with the Devil](/wiki/Race_with_the_Devil "Race with the Devil")*.

## Later work

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Later work")]

Parker made her [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") debut in 1968 in *Woman is My Idea*, written and directed by Don C. Liljenquist. In 1969, she played the title role in an [Off-Broadway](/wiki/Off-Broadway "Off-Broadway") production of [Frank Wedekind](/wiki/Frank_Wedekind "Frank Wedekind")'s [*Lulu*](/wiki/Earth_Spirit_(play) "Earth Spirit (play)").[[3]](#cite_note-3) She also appeared in the [off-Broadway](/wiki/Off-Broadway "Off-Broadway") production of *A Gun Play*.

In 1998, Parker published a novel, *[Angelique's Descent](/wiki/Angelique%27s_Descent "Angelique's Descent")*. Its sequel, *Dark Shadows: The Salem Branch*, came out in July 2006, and *Dark Shadows: Wolf Moon Rising* was released in August 2013. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Her fourth and final novel, *Heiress of Collinwood* was released in November 2016. She later reprised the role of Angelique for a new series of [*Dark Shadows* audio dramas](/wiki/Dark_Shadows_(audio_drama) "Dark Shadows (audio drama)"). She was also the reader of the unabridged audiobook recordings of her first three novels: *Angelique's Descent*, *Dark Shadows: The Salem Branch* and *Dark Shadows: Wolf Moon Rising*.

In 2012, Parker had a cameo role in [Tim Burton](/wiki/Tim_Burton "Tim Burton")'s movie version of *[Dark Shadows](/wiki/Dark_Shadows_(film) "Dark Shadows (film)")*. Later she was reunited with her *Dark Shadows* co-stars [Jerry Lacy](/wiki/Jerry_Lacy "Jerry Lacy") and [Kathryn Leigh Scott](/wiki/Kathryn_Leigh_Scott "Kathryn Leigh Scott") in two feature films about *[Doctor Mabuse](/wiki/Dr._Mabuse "Dr. Mabuse")* written and directed by Ansel Faraj. The first film *Doctor Mabuse* was released in 2013, followed by a 2014 sequel called *Doctor Mabuse: Etiopomar*.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Her final film, *The Great Nick D* (shot in 2023), is co-written and co-directed by Ansel Faraj and Nathan Wilson and co-stars her *Dark Shadows* co-stars Kathryn Leigh Scott and David Selby.

## Education and post-acting career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education and post-acting career")]

Parker attended [Central High School](/wiki/Central_High_School_(Memphis,_Tennessee) "Central High School (Memphis, Tennessee)") in Memphis and won a scholarship to Vassar College. At Vassar, she began a major in philosophy, which she completed at Southwestern at Memphis (now Rhodes College), receiving her BA. She attended graduate school at the University of Iowa and completed all course work on a Masters in speech and drama. She was working on her thesis while beginning her acting career. After retiring from acting, Parker became a high school and English teacher and obtained her MFA in creative writing from [Antioch University](/wiki/Antioch_University "Antioch University").

## Personal life and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Personal life and death")]

Parker was married to Jim Hawkins, a building contractor. They had a daughter, Caitlin. Parker had two sons, Rick and Andy, both from her first marriage to artist Tom Parker: Rick was a successful record producer, Andy a contractor. Caitlin Hawkins is a set designer and stylist for videos and commercials.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
Parker's son Rick is married to singer [Miranda Lee Richards](/wiki/Miranda_Lee_Richards "Miranda Lee Richards").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

On October 12, 2023, following a battle with cancer, Parker died in her sleep in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") at the age of 84.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Lara_Parker&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Works")]

* *[Angelique's Descent](/wiki/Angelique%27s_Descent "Angelique's Descent")*
* *The Salem Branch*
* *Wolf Moon Rising*
* *Return to Collinwood* (Introduction only)
* *Dreams of the Dark* (Introduction only)
* *Heiress of Collinwood*
